mentions.role_in_text: Developer of the Resonance software platform and QruiseML.
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Today, Qruise announced the release of Resonance, a new software platform for modelling and enhancing magnetic resonance (MR) systems.

With today's launch of Resonance, we are unveiling the first milestone in Qruise's strategic roadmap: creating a general-purpose AI Physicist that can tackle any R&D field defined by challenging physics,
The impact of Qruise's approach is already visible: RF pulses designed using Resonance have demonstrated greater spectral and spatial selectivity and higher robustness than standard block pulses.
We look forward to collaborating with academic labs, startups, and established companies to push magnetic resonance technology forward.

Qruise has completed a proof-of-concept integration of its automated bring-up software, QruiseOS, at the Israeli Quantum Computing Center (IQCC), where Quantum Machines' control systems underpin key elements of the control infrastructure.

mentions.role_in_text: completed a proof-of-concept integration of its automated bring-up software
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Qruise has completed a proof-of-concept integration of its automated bring-up software, QruiseOS, at the Israeli Quantum Computing Center (IQCC), where Quantum Machines' control systems underpin key elements of the control infrastructure.

mentions.role_in_text: Collaborated with IQCC to explore automated calibration workflows
mentions.occurrence_sentence: With this integration, Qruise and IQCC collaborated to explore automated calibration workflows of the Contralto QPU, including single-qubit bring-up followed by experiments such as CZ flux chevron, conditional phase and local phase optimisations, and interleaved randomised benchmarking.

“As we expand the IQCC testbed, we are proud to provide a platform where companies like Qruise can develop and validate their software on real, uncalibrated quantum systems, while benefiting from the support of our expert team,” said Nir Alfasi, General Manager at IQCC.

We now have a very comprehensive set of examples covering everything from generic two-level systems to superconducting qubits, trapped ions, spin qubits, and nitrogen vacancy centres in diamond. We now also have detailed tutorials guiding you through the process of creating a complete digital twin of your quantum control stack.
As for QruiseML, we've been working very hard on the next major release, so we don't have anything for you
